timeline.note: Fly-tipping reported via FixMyStreet app. Photos attached showing mattresses, bin bags, and building materials dumped on pavement.
Auto-routed to Veolia waste team based on report category.
3 additional reports linked to same location within 24 hours.
Veolia crew attended. Waste cleared. Noted branded packaging from nearby commercial premises.
Evidence of commercial origin identified in waste. Photographs taken. NOT referred to enforcement team.
2 further reports of new fly-tipping at same location. Recurrence pattern established.
Awaiting CCTV footage request from highways CCTV team. Request submitted 7 April.

case_notes: Persistent fly-tipping hotspot on Lisson Grove near Church Street estate. Cleared once on 2 April but recurred within 6 days. Evidence of commercial waste origin found during clearance (branded packaging from food business) but was NOT referred to the enforcement team for investigation - this is a process failure. 5 duplicate reports from residents indicate high community impact. CCTV footage requested but not yet received. 48-hour clearance SLA was breached on initial report (cleared day 5). Recurrence suggests need for enforcement action and possible covert monitoring.

timeline.note: Pothole reported with attached photo. Reporter states cyclist was injured at this location yesterday. Pothole approximately 30cm wide and 10cm deep in cycle lane.
Injury report attached. System flagged for urgent triage.
1 additional report received same day referencing the cyclist incident.

case_notes: Dangerous pothole in cycle lane on Westbourne Grove near junction with Chepstow Road. Cyclist injury reported - reporter states they witnessed the incident and cyclist was taken to St Mary's A&E. Pothole is approximately 30cm wide and 10cm deep. Located in a busy cycle commuter route. This should be triaged as Category 1 (safety hazard requiring 2-hour response) due to the injury report, but has NOT been triaged or assigned a category yet after 48 hours. Two reports received. FM Conway highways team has been auto-assigned but no inspection or make-safe action logged.

timeline.note: Hate-related graffiti reported on residential wall facing primary school entrance. Offensive racial slurs and symbols.
Auto-escalated to urgent priority due to hate-related content classification.
Metropolitan Police notified via standard hate crime reporting channel. Crime ref: CAD-5591/26.
2 further reports from parents at adjacent school. Councillor enquiry received from Cllr Meredith.
Councillor enquiry logged. Response due within 5 working days (by 16 April).
Escalated to area manager. 24-hour SLA for hate-related graffiti breached by 10 days. Councillor enquiry response overdue in 2 days.

case_notes: Hate-related graffiti on residential wall on Lupus Street, directly facing Churchill Gardens Primary School entrance. Content includes racial slurs and offensive symbols. Police notified (CAD-5591/26). 24-hour removal SLA for hate-related graffiti has been breached by 10 days - this is a serious service failure. Property owner has been contacted but removal requires specialist paint-over due to surface type. Councillor Meredith has raised this directly - response due by 16 April. 3 reports from community, including parents concerned about children seeing the content. Escalated to area manager for immediate resolution.

timeline.note: Area street lighting outage reported. 4 consecutive lamp columns not working on York Street between Seymour Place and Wyndham Place.
Routed to Citelum lighting maintenance team.
Citelum inspected. Identified supply cable fault affecting 4 columns. Requires UKPN coordination for excavation.
UKPN excavation permit requested. Estimated 10 working days for approval.
UKPN permit still pending. 21 days since report. Approaching double-SLA threshold.

case_notes: Area outage affecting 4 lamp columns on York Street. Supply cable fault identified by Citelum - not a simple bulb replacement. Requires UKPN coordination for excavation work. UKPN permit requested 1 April but not yet approved. Standard lighting SLA is 14 working days for non-emergency repairs. Now at 26 days (approaching double-SLA escalation threshold of 28 working days). Resident concern about pedestrian safety on unlit section, particularly given proximity to Marylebone station with high evening foot traffic.

timeline.note: Abandoned vehicle reported. White Ford Transit van, no plates, flat tyres, appears to have been stationary for several weeks.
Routed to NSL parking enforcement team.
NSL officer inspected. Confirmed vehicle has no plates, flat tyres, and parking ticket dated 18 March (21 days old). 7-day removal notice affixed to windscreen.
7-day notice period in progress. Vehicle to be checked again on 17 April. DVLA check submitted.

case_notes: Standard abandoned vehicle case. White Ford Transit van on Porchester Road, no registration plates, two flat tyres. NSL inspection confirmed vehicle appears abandoned. 7-day removal notice posted on 10 April - expiry 17 April. DVLA trace submitted to confirm registered keeper. If vehicle remains after notice period, removal will be arranged through contractor. Process on track, no SLA issues.

timeline.note: First noise complaint about construction site operating outside permitted hours (before 8am and after 6pm weekdays).
Section 60 notice served on site operator. Hours restricted to 8am-6pm Mon-Fri, 8am-1pm Sat.
4 further complaints received. Residents report work continuing at 7am and past 8pm.
Environmental Health officer visited at 7:15am. Confirmed breach of Section 60 notice. Formal warning issued.
6 additional complaints. Pattern of weekend working reported.
Escalated to senior EH officer. Prosecution file being prepared. 12 complaints total across 3 separate reporters.
Case file referred to legal services for prosecution under Control of Pollution Act 1974.

case_notes: Persistent noise nuisance from construction site on Praed Street near Paddington Station. Developer: Greystone Developments Ltd. Section 60 notice served 20 February but breached multiple times. Environmental Health officer confirmed breach during early morning visit on 10 March. 12 complaints from at least 8 different households across two residential blocks. Pattern of early morning starts (before 7am) and late evening working (past 9pm), plus unauthorised Saturday afternoon work. Previous prosecution history: Greystone were fined GBP 8,000 in 2024 for similar breach at a Maida Vale site. Case referred to legal services for prosecution under Control of Pollution Act 1974, section 60(8).

timeline.note: Police referral for rough sleeper welfare concern. Individual known to outreach services. Appears to have deteriorating health.
Routed to St Mungo's outreach team via StreetLink referral.
St Mungo's outreach worker attended. Individual located. Refused hostel placement but accepted food parcel and sleeping bag. Health concerns noted - persistent cough, weight loss.
Second outreach visit. Individual not found at reported location. Checked 3 known alternative locations - not located.
Follow-up visit due today. If individual located, health referral to be made to NHS outreach GP service.

case_notes: Welfare concern for known rough sleeper on The Strand, referred by Met Police. Individual is known to St Mungo's (client ref SM-4412). Previous engagements recorded over 18 months. Health concerns flagged by referring officer - persistent cough and visible weight loss. First outreach contact made 3 April - individual declined hostel but accepted support items. Not located on follow-up visit 8 April. Third visit due today (15 April). If located, plan is to make NHS outreach GP referral. Case requires sensitive handling - individual has history of disengaging from services when pressured. Welfare-priority case, not enforcement.

timeline.note: Street patrol officer identified commercial waste dumped outside licensed premises at 6:30am, outside permitted collection window.
Photographs taken. CCTV footage requested from council cameras. Waste traced to specific business via branded bags and receipts.
CCTV footage received. Shows staff member from identified premises depositing waste bags at 5:45am. Collection window is 7pm-7am - waste deposited after window closed.
Section 34 investigation notice drafted. Due to be served by 5 April.
Section 34 Environmental Protection Act 1990 investigation notice served on business. 14-day response window.
Awaiting business response to investigation notice. Response deadline: 22 April.

case_notes: Commercial waste enforcement case on Berwick Street, Soho. Street patrol officer identified waste dumped at 6:30am outside the permitted collection window. CCTV evidence confirmed staff from 'Soho Bites' restaurant depositing waste at 5:45am - technically after the overnight collection window closed at 5am (not 7am as initially noted; Soho special zone window is 8pm-5am). Section 34 investigation notice served 8 April. Business has until 22 April to respond. Note: investigation notice was due to be served by 5 April but was served 3 days late - minor process delay. Previous duty of care audit on this premises was 14 months ago with no issues. Fixed Penalty Notice of GBP 400 likely if duty of care breach confirmed.

timeline.note: Fly-tipping reported. Small amount of household furniture left on pavement (sofa and coffee table).
Routed to Veolia waste team.
Veolia crew attended. Items removed and disposed of. No evidence of commercial origin.
Case closed. Reporter notified via app. Satisfaction survey sent.

case_notes: Small-scale fly-tipping on Elgin Avenue - household furniture (sofa and coffee table). Cleared within 24 hours by Veolia. No evidence of commercial origin, no enforcement action required. Standard case resolved within SLA. Reporter notified of resolution.

timeline.note: Overflowing litter bin reported near Selfridges entrance. Waste spilling onto pavement.
Bin emptied by Veolia on scheduled round.
3 further reports of same bin overflowing.
Bin emptied again.
3 more reports. Recurring pattern established - bin overflows between 2pm-6pm on high footfall days.
Veolia area supervisor reviewed. Recommended either increasing collection frequency or replacing with larger bin.
Awaiting footfall data from highways team to justify bin replacement or schedule change. 8 reports in 4 weeks.

case_notes: Recurring overflowing bin on Oxford Street near Selfridges. 8 reports in 4 weeks from multiple reporters. Pattern: bin overflows between 2pm-6pm on busy retail days (Thursdays through Saturdays). Current collection schedule appears insufficient for this location's footfall. Veolia area supervisor has recommended either increasing collection frequency to 3x daily (from current 2x) or replacing with a BigBelly compacting bin. Awaiting footfall data from highways team to build business case for bin upgrade. This is a high-visibility location with tourist and retail footfall - reputational risk for the council if left unresolved.

timeline.note: Large-scale fly-tipping reported under Marylebone Flyover. Construction waste including plasterboard, timber, and asbestos-containing materials suspected.
Routed to Veolia waste team. Priority auto-uplifted to high due to suspected hazardous materials.
3 additional reports received. One reporter mentions seeing a white tipper truck at 5am.

Veolia crew attended. Confirmed large-scale dump approximately 8 cubic metres. Suspected asbestos-containing material identified. Environment Agency notification required. Area cordoned off with hazard tape.

case_notes: Large-scale fly-tipping under Marylebone Flyover on Edgware Road. Approximately 8 cubic metres of construction waste including suspected asbestos-containing materials. Large-scale clearance SLA is 24 hours but hazardous materials require Environment Agency notification first. Site cordoned off. CCTV request submitted to TfL for flyover cameras. Witness report of white tipper truck at 5am on 12 April. Environment Agency notified but clearance delayed pending their assessment. 24-hour SLA likely to be breached due to hazmat protocol.

timeline.note: Pothole reported on Sutherland Avenue near junction with Castellain Road. Approximately 20cm wide in nearside lane.
FM Conway inspection booked for 4 April.
Inspected and categorised as Category 2. 20cm x 15cm, depth 6cm. Repair scheduled within 7 working days.
Permanent repair scheduled for 16 April. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard Category 2 pothole on Sutherland Avenue. Inspected and measured at 20cm x 15cm, 6cm deep. Not a safety hazard but will deteriorate if not repaired. Permanent repair scheduled for 16 April, within 7 working day SLA. Process on track.

timeline.note: Non-offensive graffiti tags on council-owned wall on Wardour Street. Multiple spray paint tags across approximately 3 metres.
Assigned to Veolia cleansing team. Council property confirmed.
Graffiti removed by Veolia. Before and after photos logged.
Case closed. Reporter notified. Completed within 10 working day SLA.

case_notes: Non-offensive graffiti tags on council wall on Wardour Street. Standard removal by Veolia within 10 working day SLA. Case closed satisfactorily.

timeline.note: Noise complaint from residential flat above nightclub on Dean Street. Bass music audible until 3am, vibrating through floors.
2 additional complaints from same building. One from adjacent building.
Environmental Health officer visited at midnight. Confirmed excessive bass noise levels. Sound limiter appears to have been tampered with.
2 more complaints received over weekend. Total 5 reporters.
Checking licensing conditions and previous noise history. Premises has had 2 previous complaints in 2025, both resolved informally.

case_notes: Persistent bass noise from nightclub 'Velvet Underground' on Dean Street affecting residents in flats above and adjacent building. Environmental Health visit confirmed sound limiter may have been tampered with - this would be a breach of licensing condition 14 (sound limiter to be maintained at calibrated level). 5 complaints in one week from at least 4 separate households. Classified as persistent nuisance (3+ complaints in 30 days). Cross-referral to licensing team needed but NOT yet actioned. Previous informal warnings in 2025 had temporary effect.

timeline.note: Abandoned car reported on Queensway. Silver Honda Civic, no tax disc visible, flat rear tyre.
NSL officer inspected. Confirmed 2 indicators of abandonment. 7-day notice affixed.
Notice period expired. Vehicle still present. DVLA trace returned - vehicle untaxed since January 2026.
Removal arranged via contractor. Scheduled for 1 April.
Vehicle removed by contractor. Taken to compound. 14-day holding period commenced.
No claim from registered keeper. Vehicle disposed of. Case closed.

case_notes: Standard abandoned vehicle removal case. Silver Honda Civic on Queensway, untaxed since January 2026. Full process followed: inspection, 7-day notice, DVLA trace, removal, 14-day holding period. No claim. Vehicle disposed of. Completed within standard timescales.

timeline.note: Fly-tipping reported on Harrow Road near Westbourne Park station. Commercial waste bags from multiple food businesses.
Assigned to Veolia for clearance.
Waste cleared. Evidence found - branded packaging from 3 identifiable businesses.
Evidence photographed and referred to waste enforcement team within 24 hours.
Recurrence at same location. 4 reports in 10 days. Councillor enquiry from Cllr Iqbal.
Councillor enquiry logged. Response provided within 5 working days.
Fixed Penalty Notices (GBP 400 each) issued to 2 of the 3 identified businesses. Third business cooperated and provided waste transfer notes.
One FPN paid. Second business has not paid within 14 days - prosecution referral being prepared.

case_notes: Persistent commercial fly-tipping on Harrow Road linked to food businesses near Westbourne Park station. Evidence gathered and referred correctly. FPNs issued to 2 businesses. One paid, one outstanding past 14-day payment window - prosecution referral in preparation. Councillor enquiry responded to within SLA. Recurrence pattern may require covert monitoring installation.

timeline.note: Single lamp column not working on Connaught Street outside number 14.
Routed to Citelum.
Citelum inspected. Faulty photocell identified. Part ordered.
Photocell replaced. Lamp confirmed working. Case closed within 14 working day SLA.

case_notes: Single lamp outage on Connaught Street. Simple photocell fault identified and repaired by Citelum within 14 working day SLA. Standard case, no issues.

timeline.note: Overflowing public waste bin at Church Street Market. Waste spilling around bin and onto market stalls.
Assigned to Veolia. Added to afternoon round.
Bin emptied on morning round. Area around bin cleaned.

case_notes: Overflowing bin at Church Street Market. Emptied on next scheduled round. No recurring pattern detected. Standard resolution.

timeline.note: Rough sleeper welfare concern. Female, appears confused and disoriented, sleeping in doorway on Victoria Street.
St Mungo's outreach worker attended. Individual located and engaged. Known to services. Agreed to come to assessment hub.
Individual attended assessment hub. Housing options being explored. Mental health referral made to CNWL.
Temporary accommodation secured via Westminster Housing Solutions. Individual moved in.
Follow-up visit confirmed individual settled in temporary accommodation. Support plan in place. Case closed.

case_notes: Positive outcome. Rough sleeping welfare case on Victoria Street. Individual engaged with outreach, attended assessment hub, mental health referral made, and temporary accommodation secured. Support plan established with CNWL and housing support worker. Case closed with successful housing outcome.

timeline.note: Multiple businesses on Old Compton Street depositing waste bags outside after 5am collection window. Soho special zone rules apply.
Photographs taken at 6:15am showing waste from 4 premises. Bags identifiable by branded packaging.
Section 34 investigation notices being prepared for 4 businesses. CCTV footage requested. Due to be served by 17 April.

case_notes: Multiple commercial waste duty of care investigation on Old Compton Street, Soho. Four businesses identified depositing waste outside the Soho special zone collection window (8pm-5am). Photographic evidence collected. Section 34 notices being prepared. This area has been subject to enhanced enforcement due to persistent non-compliance issues.

timeline.note: Minor pothole reported on Baker Street near Marylebone Road junction.
Inspected. Category 3 minor defect. 15cm, depth 3cm. Scheduled for routine repair programme.
Repair included in Baker Street area maintenance programme.
Repaired as part of area maintenance. Reporter notified. Completed within 28 working day SLA.

case_notes: Minor Category 3 pothole on Baker Street. Repaired as part of scheduled area maintenance programme. Completed well within 28 working day SLA. No issues.

timeline.note: Non-offensive graffiti on private commercial property wall on Vauxhall Bridge Road. Large mural-style tag.
Assigned to Veolia. Confirmed as private property - owner notification required.
Property owner notified. 14-day window for self-removal (deadline 22 April). If not removed, council may serve defacement removal notice.

case_notes: Non-offensive graffiti on private commercial property on Vauxhall Bridge Road. Owner notification sent with 14-day removal window ending 22 April. Standard process. If owner does not remove, defacement removal notice may be considered under Anti-social Behaviour Act 2003.

timeline.note: Noise complaint about residential flat above. Loud music and parties on Friday and Saturday nights.
Initial letter sent to occupant of flat. Noise diary provided to complainant.
Environmental Health officer attended on Saturday at 11pm. No excessive noise detected on this occasion.
No further complaints received in 2 weeks. Letter appears to have resolved issue. Case closed - to be reopened if complaints resume.

case_notes: Residential noise complaint on Gloucester Place. Initial advisory letter appears to have resolved the issue. No excessive noise detected during officer visit. No further complaints in 2 weeks. Case closed. Complainant advised to contact again if noise resumes.

timeline.note: Fly-tipping reported on Shirland Road near canal bridge. Household waste and garden refuse.
Veolia cleared waste within 48 hours.
3 reports of new fly-tipping at same spot. Recurrence pattern.
Cleared again.
4 more reports. 7 total now.
Cleared third time.
4 more reports. 11 total. Formal service review triggered.
Escalated to area manager. 11 reports in 54 days triggers formal service review. Covert monitoring recommended.

case_notes: Persistent fly-tipping hotspot on Shirland Road near canal bridge. 11 reports in 54 days. Cleared 3 times but recurs within 2 weeks each time. 10+ reports in 30 days threshold breached - formal service review triggered. Escalated to area manager. Covert camera monitoring recommended to identify perpetrators. Location is partially hidden from main road, creating opportunity for dumping.

timeline.note: Area outage affecting 5 lamp columns on Inverness Terrace. Dark section approximately 100 metres.
Routed to Citelum. High priority due to area outage.
Citelum inspected. Underground cable fault requiring excavation. UKPN coordination needed.
UKPN permit submitted. Estimated 3-4 week lead time.
Cllr Chen raises safety concerns. Multiple residents complained at ward surgery about dark street. Response due within 5 working days.
Response sent to Cllr Chen within SLA. Explained UKPN dependency.
45 days since report. Double-SLA threshold (10 working days for area outage) breached. Escalated to highways manager. Written explanation from Citelum requested. Pedestrian safety assessment overdue.

case_notes: Area outage on Inverness Terrace affecting 5 lamp columns. Underground cable fault requiring UKPN excavation. 45 days since report - well past double-SLA threshold of 10 working days for area outage. UKPN permit still pending. Councillor enquiry responded to within SLA but underlying issue unresolved. Pedestrian safety assessment should have been conducted at 7 days but was NOT done - process failure. Escalated to highways manager. Dark street section is adjacent to Queensway tube station with high evening pedestrian traffic.

timeline.note: Commercial waste deposited outside premises on Great Windmill Street at 6am, outside Soho special zone window.
Photographs taken. Business identified.
Section 34 investigation notice served on business.
Business responded within 14 days. Provided valid waste transfer notes. Explanation: new staff member unaware of special zone hours.
Evidence reviewed. First offence, valid documentation provided, remedial action taken (staff training).
Advisory letter issued. No FPN given first offence with valid documentation. Duty of care audit scheduled for 6 months. Case closed.

case_notes: Commercial waste case on Great Windmill Street. First offence - business provided valid waste transfer notes and implemented staff training. Advisory letter issued, no FPN. Duty of care audit scheduled for September 2026. Positive resolution.

timeline.note: Large pothole reported on Sussex Gardens near Paddington station. Reporter states vehicles swerving to avoid it.
Inspected. Category 2, approximately 35cm wide, 8cm deep. Repair scheduled within 7 working days.
Repair scheduled but cancelled due to utility works by Thames Water on same section.
Thames Water works still ongoing. Repair rescheduled. SLA breached.
3 further complaints. Pothole has worsened - now approximately 40cm wide.
44 days since report. SLA breached by 37 days. Escalated to highways manager. Defect has worsened. Thames Water works creating access issues.

case_notes: Category 2 pothole on Sussex Gardens that has worsened due to delayed repair. Original 7 working day SLA breached by 37 days. Repair delayed by Thames Water utility works on same section of road. Pothole has grown from 35cm to 40cm. 3 additional complaints. Escalated to highways manager - coordination with Thames Water needed to schedule repair during or immediately after their works.

timeline.note: Overflowing bin near Green Park station exit on Piccadilly.
Bin emptied.
3 more reports. Same bin overflowing.
Bin emptied again.
3 more reports. 6 total. Recurring pattern confirmed.
Bin emptied.
Capacity review initiated. Location is high-footfall tourist area near Green Park station and the Ritz. BigBelly compactor recommended.

case_notes: Recurring overflowing bin near Green Park station on Piccadilly. 6 reports in 4 weeks. High tourist footfall area between Green Park station and the Ritz. Capacity review underway - BigBelly compactor strongly recommended given location. Veolia have proposed 3x daily collection as interim measure pending bin upgrade.

timeline.note: Non-offensive graffiti on council-owned electricity substation on Marylebone Lane. Spray paint tags.
Assigned to Veolia cleansing team. Council property confirmed. 10 working day SLA.

case_notes: Non-offensive graffiti tags on council-owned substation on Marylebone Lane. Assigned to Veolia for removal within 10 working day SLA. Standard case.

timeline.note: Noise complaint about restaurant kitchen extraction fan operating 24 hours. Low frequency hum affecting sleep.
Initial investigation. EH officer visited. Confirmed low frequency noise from extraction unit running continuously.
2 additional complaints from adjacent flats.
Abatement notice served under EPA 1990 s.80 requiring noise reduction measures within 21 days (by 26 April).
Restaurant owner has engaged acoustic consultant. Proposed anti-vibration mounts and timer control to limit overnight operation.

case_notes: Low frequency noise nuisance from restaurant extraction fan on Tachbrook Street. Running 24 hours including overnight. 3 complaints from residents in flats above. Abatement notice served with 21-day compliance deadline (26 April). Restaurant owner cooperating - acoustic consultant engaged and solution proposed (anti-vibration mounts + timer). Compliance expected before deadline.

timeline.note: street lighting reported at Victoria Street.
Routed to Citelum.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard street lighting case at Victoria Street, SW1E 5ND. Processing in progress.

case_notes: Standard street lighting case at Palmer Street, SW1H 0PH. Processing in progress.

Routed to Citelum.

case_notes: Standard street lighting case at Churton Street, SW1V 2LP. Processing in progress.

Routed to WCC (in-house).
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard noise complaint case at Marsham Street, SW1P 4DF. Processing in progress.

Routed to FM Conway.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard pothole case at Strutton Ground, SW1P 2HR. Processing in progress.

Routed to FM Conway.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard pothole case at Victoria Street, SW1E 5ND. Processing in progress.

Routed to Veolia.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard graffiti case at Broadway, SW1H 0BG. Processing in progress.

Routed to FM Conway.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.
Issue resolved. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard pothole case at Horseferry Road, SW1P 2AF. Resolved within SLA.

Routed to NSL.

case_notes: Standard abandoned vehicle case at Caxton Street, SW1H 0PZ. Processing in progress.

Routed to Veolia.

case_notes: Standard fly tipping case at Tachbrook Street, SW1V 2NA. Processing in progress.

Routed to WCC (in-house).
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.
Issue resolved. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard noise complaint case at Vauxhall Bridge Road, SW1V 2SA. Resolved within SLA.

Routed to WCC (in-house).

case_notes: Standard commercial waste case at Palmer Street, SW1H 0PH. Processing in progress.

timeline.note: street lighting reported at Vauxhall Bridge Road.
Routed to Citelum.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.
Issue resolved. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard street lighting case at Vauxhall Bridge Road, SW1V 2SA. Resolved within SLA.

Routed to NSL.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard abandoned vehicle case at Palmer Street, SW1H 0PH. Processing in progress.

Routed to FM Conway.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.
Issue resolved. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard pothole case at Abbey Orchard Street, SW1P 2JJ. Resolved within SLA.

timeline.note: commercial waste reported at Regency Street.
Routed to WCC (in-house).
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: Standard commercial waste case at Regency Street, SW1P 4BH. Processing in progress.

timeline.note: overflowing bin reported at Broadway.
Routed to Veolia.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.
Issue resolved. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard overflowing bin case at Broadway, SW1H 0BG. Processing in progress.

timeline.note: pothole reported at Rochester Row.
Routed to FM Conway.
Officer attended and assessed. Photographs taken.
Issue resolved. Reporter notified.

case_notes: Standard pothole case at Rochester Row, SW1P 1JU. Resolved within SLA.

Routed to Veolia.

case_notes: Standard fly tipping case at Warwick Way, SW1V 1QT. Processing in progress.

Routed to Veolia.

case_notes: Standard fly tipping case at Buckingham Palace Road, SW1W 0QP. Processing in progress.
